entry.title: Bitcoin Core 30.3 released
entry.summary:
The latest iteration of Bitcoin Core, version 30.3, is now available for download at [BitcoinCore.org](https://bitcoincore.org/bin/bitcoin-core-30.3/). This release heralds various enhancements including new features, bug fixes, performance improvements, and updated translations. Users encountering issues are encouraged to use the GitHub issue tracker to report bugs at [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues) or join the announcements list at [Bitcoin Core Announcements](https://bitcoincore.org/en/list/announcements/join/).
For those upgrading from an older version of Bitcoin Core, it is advised to shut down the existing version completely before installation. This may take a few minutes depending on the system and setup. The upgrade process varies slightly between operating systems: Windows users should run the installer, macOS users should overwrite `/Applications/Bitcoin-Qt`, and Linux users should replace `bitcoind`/`bitcoin-qt`. Even versions that have reached their end-of-life (EOL) can be upgraded, although migrating data directories might extend the upgrade duration. It’s also worth noting that older wallet versions are generally supported.
This version is compatible with Linux Kernel versions 3.17 and above, macOS 13 and above, and Windows 10 and above. While Bitcoin Core may operate on other Unix-like systems, such instances are less frequently tested and using Bitcoin Core on unsupported systems is not recommended.
Significant changes in this release include a fix to an issue in the chainstate database where large portions were being rewritten repeatedly, causing excessive disk operations. There are several updates across different components such as validation corrections, wallet bug fixes, improvements in handling transaction fees and coin selection, network interface adjustments, and build configuration updates among others. Notably, there have been numerous contributions towards improving the testing environment and continuous integration (CI) configurations which enhance the overall development process.
Contributors to this release include a wide range of developers whose efforts continue to support and enhance the functionality of Bitcoin Core. Their involvement is crucial for the ongoing development and maintenance of this critical piece of Bitcoin infrastructure.
